title: Women In Prison: How it is With Us
description: Assata Shakur's reflection on the conditions of women in U.S. prisons. Reproduced as part of the Boston ABC zine distro.

title: An Updated History of the New Afrikan Prison Struggle
description: Sundiata Acoli's updated history of the New Afrikan prison struggle, tracing Black political prisoner organizing inside U.S. prisons.

title: Still Black Still Strong
description: Semiotext(e) collection on Black liberation politics and political prisoners, distributed by Boston ABC.

title: Survival Guide for the Ex-Prisoner
description: Practical guide by Khalfani X. Khaldun for people transitioning out of incarceration.

title: Reflections on Women in Prison
description: Reflections on the experience of women in prison by Summer Thomas and Jenn Bowman.

title: Prisons and Their Moral Influence on Prisoners
description: Kropotkin's classic anarchist critique of the moral effects of prison on incarcerated people.

title: The Prison-Industrial Complex and the Global Economy
description: Eve Goldberg's analysis tying the prison-industrial complex to global capitalist economic structures.

title: Political Prisoners, Prisons and Black Liberation
description: Angela Davis's foundational essay on political prisoners, the carceral state, and Black liberation.

title: Parents in Prison: A Growing Crisis
description: Austin ABC zine on the impact of incarceration on parents and families.

title: No Prisoner Left Behind: The Fight for Transgender/Gender Variant Prisoners
description: Kevin Weaver on the specific struggles of transgender and gender-variant prisoners and the fight for their rights.

title: Celling Black Bodies: Black Women in the Prison Industrial Complex
description: Julia Sudbury on the intersection of race, gender, and the prison-industrial complex.

title: Black People's Prison Survival Guide
description: Abdullah Ibraheem's practical guide to surviving prison as a Black incarcerated person.

title: It's Like Living in a Black Hole: Women of Color and Solitary Confinement
description: Cassandra Shaylor on the impact of solitary confinement on women of color in U.S. prisons.

title: On Law and Authority
description: Kropotkin's classic anarchist critique of law and authority as social institutions.

title: Non-Western Anarchisms: Rethinking the Global Context
description: Jason Adams's survey of anarchist movements outside the Euro-American context.

title: Notes on Anarchism
description: Chomsky's introductory notes on anarchist political thought.

title: The Capitalist System
description: Bakunin's analysis of capitalism and exploitation, reproduced as an anarchist classic.

title: What is Syndicalism?
description: Emma Goldman's primer on syndicalism as a revolutionary labor strategy.

title: Black Anarchism
description: Ashanti Alston's foundational essay on Black anarchism and its relationship to Black liberation.

title: Prisons: A Social Crime and Failure
description: Emma Goldman's essay denouncing prisons as a social crime and failure.

title: Anarchy: A Pamphlet
description: Errico Malatesta's classic introduction to anarchist ideas.

title: Crime and Punishment
description: Voltairine De Cleyre's anarchist critique of crime and punishment.

title: Lessons From Cointelpro: Building a Movement in the Face of Repression
description: Claude Marks and Kelah Bott on lessons from COINTELPRO for movement-building under repression.

title: Law Enforcement Violence Against Women of Color
description: Andrea Ritchie on patterns of police and law-enforcement violence against women of color.

title: Invisibility of Women Prisoner Resistance
description: Vikki Law on the historical invisibility of women prisoners' resistance within prison struggles.

title: Joint Statement on Gender Violence and the Prison Industrial Complex
description: Joint statement by Incite! and Critical Resistance addressing the relationship between gender violence and the prison-industrial complex.

title: Prison, Where is Thy Victory?
description: Huey P. Newton's classic essay on the politics of imprisonment and Black liberation.

title: History of the Anarchist Red Cross
description: Boris Yelensky's account of the origins of the Anarchist Red Cross, predecessor to the Anarchist Black Cross.

title: Handbook on Surviving Solitary Confinement
description: Khalifani Malik Khaldun's handbook for prisoners enduring solitary confinement.

title: Anarchist Survival Guide for Understanding Gestapo Swine Interrogation Mind Games
description: Harold H. Thompson's guide to recognizing and resisting police interrogation tactics.

title: Control Unit Prisons
description: Frank Atwood on the development and operation of control-unit prisons in the U.S.

title: Dykes and Fags Want to Know: Interview With Lesbian Political Prisoners
authors: Linda Evans, Laura Whitehorn & Susan Rosenberg
description: Interview with lesbian political prisoners Linda Evans, Laura Whitehorn, and Susan Rosenberg.

title: Democracy is Direct
description: Cindy Milstein's argument for direct democracy as an anarchist political form.

title: How to Use the Law Library and Write Your Law Work
description: D. Michael Salerno's guide for prisoners on conducting legal research and writing legal motions.

title: Attacking Prisons at the Point of Production
description: Collected writings on attacking prisons at the point of production through labor disruption.

title: Anarchism and Litigation
description: Eric D. Smith on the contradictions between anarchist principles and the use of litigation.

title: We Are Being Doxxed: What To Do To Help Keep Each Other Safe
description: Digital Defense primer on how activists can protect themselves and each other from doxxing.

title: Surviving a Grand Jury
description: Crimethinc's guide for activists facing a federal grand jury.

title: Resist Grand Juries
description: 1312 Press pamphlet on grand jury resistance.

title: Soaring Beyond the Walls: Tools for Building Capacity in Prison and Beyond
description: Somatics-focused tools for building emotional and movement capacity inside and outside prison.

title: Cameras Everywhere Safety Nowhere
description: Crimethinc critique of surveillance culture and its impact on public safety and movement security.

title: Everybody Hates the Police
description: Anonymous zine compiling anti-police writings and arguments.

title: An Activist's Guide to Basic First Aid
description: Black Cross Health Collective primer on first aid for street medics and activists.

title: Class Struggle & Mental Health: Live to Fight Another Day
description: Anthology connecting class struggle politics with mental health and movement sustainability.

title: Know Your Rights
description: Natasha Lennard's know-your-rights primer for protestors.

title: An Unprecedented Case
description: Li2 essay on an unprecedented federal case and its implications for movements.

title: The Police Threw the Book at Protestors in DC but Sat and Watched White Supremacists Terrorize Charlottesville
description: Anonymous essay contrasting police treatment of DC J20 protestors with their inaction in Charlottesville.

title: Why I Am Facing 75 Years
description: First-person statement by J20 defendant Carlo Piantini on the charges he faced.

title: How the Government is Turning Protestors into Felons
description: Natasha Lennard on the federal government's prosecution of protestors as felons.

title: It's a Police State Mentality
description: Sam Alder-Bell analyzes the expansion of police-state thinking in U.S. politics.

title: What About the Workers
description: James Boggs on Black workers and revolutionary politics.

title: Collective Process
description: Common Wheel Collective's guide to running collective decision-making processes.

title: Stay Healthy So You Can Stay in the Streets
description: Boston Area Liberation Medic Squad guide to staying healthy through sustained street protest.

title: Supporting a Survivor of Sexual Assault
description: Men Against Rape Culture and UBUNTU practical guide to supporting survivors of sexual assault.

title: Policing on the Global Scale
description: Venomous Butterfly Publications on the globalization of policing.

title: Fight the Man and Get Away Safely
description: Anonymous guide to militant action and safe escape.

title: Political Prisoners, Prisons, and Black Liberation (2018 reprint)
description: Angela Y. Davis's foundational essay on political prisoners and Black liberation; 2018 Boston ABC reprint.

title: What is Security Culture?
description: Sprout Distro Collective and Crimethinc introduction to security culture for activist communities.

title: Tasers and the Fight Against Police Brutality
description: Sallydarity's critique of taser use in policing and the broader fight against police brutality.

title: The Fundamental Requirement for Organised Safer Space
description: Anarchist Federation discussion of building organised safer space in radical organising.

title: Learning Good Consent
description: Anonymous anthology of essays and reflections on practicing consent in radical communities.

title: Come Hell or High Water: Handbook on Collective Process Gone Awry
description: Delfina Vanucci and Richard Singer's handbook on diagnosing and repairing dysfunctional collective process.

title: Consensus
description: Peter Gelderloos's primer on consensus decision-making for radical organising.

title: If An Agent Knocks
description: Center for Constitutional Rights know-your-rights guide on dealing with federal agents, hosted by Boston ABC as a printable resource.

title: Si Un Agente Golpea (If An Agent Knocks - Spanish)
description: Spanish-language Center for Constitutional Rights know-your-rights guide on dealing with federal agents, hosted by Boston ABC.

title: The J20 Arrests and Trials Explained
description: Zine explaining the mass arrest of 230 people at the Trump inauguration protests on January 20, 2017, the subsequent federal riot charges, defendant solidarity, and the eventual dismissal of all remaining charges by July 2018.

title: Starting An Anarchist Black Cross Group: A Guide
description: Boston ABC's how-to guide for establishing a local Anarchist Black Cross prisoner-support group.

title: Prison Books Resource Sheet
description: Boston ABC resource sheet listing prison-books programs and projects.

title: Boston ABC Info Letter (Printable Format)
description: Printable Boston ABC information letter for sending to prisoners or new contacts.

title: Security Culture Slides v.01
description: Slide deck used by Boston ABC for security-culture trainings.

title: Security Culture Introduction
description: Boston ABC introductory handout on security culture concepts and practices.

title: Prison Action News - Envelope Template
description: Printable envelope template used to mail Prison Action News to incarcerated subscribers.
